questions.question:
Which of the following is the derivative of \( f(x)=x^{x} \) when \( x>0 \) ?



Calculus: TIFR CSE 2018 | Part A | Question: 5
options: \( \text{None of the above; function is not differentiable for }x>0 \)

questions.question:
Suppose \( w(t)=4 e^{i t}, x(t)=3 e^{i(t+\pi / 3)}, y(t)=3 e^{i(t-\pi / 3)} \) and \( z(t)=3 e^{i(t+\pi)} \) are points that move in the complex plane as the time \( t \) varies in \( (-\infty, \infty) \). Let \( c(t) \) be the point in the complex plane such that \( |w(t)-c(t)|^2+|x(t)-c(t)|^2+|y(t)-c(t)|^2+|z(t)-c(t)|^2 \) is minimum. For each value of \( t \), the point \( c(t) \) is unique, but \( c(t) \) moves at constant speed as \( t \) varies. At what speed? That is, what is \( \left|\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} t} c(t)\right|? \) 



Calculus: TIFR CSE 2022 | Part A | Question: 14

Let \( f(x)=a x^{3}+b x^{2}+c x+d \) be a polynomial, where \( a, b, c, d \) are unknown real numbers. It is further given that \( f(1)=1, f(2)=2, f(3)=9 \), and \( f^{\prime}(1)=0 \). Then, the value of \( f^{\prime}(2) \) must be



Calculus: TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 14
options: \( f^{\prime}(2) \) cannot be determined uniquely from the information given in the question.
